d56d96944b5b.original_description:
Andy Jassy tells shareholders that long-awaited rival to Elon Musk’s Starlink is ‘on the verge’ of going live

Amazon has said its long-awaited satellite internet rival to Elon Musk’s Starlink will finally go live in “mid-2026”.

The chief executive, Andy Jassy, said in a letter to shareholders that the technology company was “on the verge of launching Amazon Leo” and had secured “revenue commitments from enterprises and governments” for the scheme.

417311dddc7b.title: Genetics may help explain why results from weight-loss jabs vary, say scientists
417311dddc7b.summary:
Data on almost 28,000 patients suggests understanding gene variations could improve treatments for obesity

Scientists have discovered how genetics may help explain why weight-loss jabs work better for some people than others.

Variations in two genes involved in gut hormone pathways, which regulate appetite and digestion, may help account for different weight-loss results or side-effects when taking glucagon-like peptide 1 (GLP1) medicines.

3c716cfa4a66.title: Can we eradicate a second human disease? – podcast
3c716cfa4a66.summary: Согласно данным Carter Centre, количество случаев болезни говяжьей воронки достигло исторического минимума - всего 10 случаев в прошлом году. Несмотря на значительное сокращение заболеваемости, полное искоренение паразита остается сложной задачей, ведь только оспа была полностью искоренена. 3c716cfa4a66.original_description: The number of human cases of guinea worm, a painful and debilitating tropical illness, fell to a record low of just 10 last year, according to the Carter Centre, the foundation set up by the late former US president Jimmy Carter. But despite years-long declines, it remains almost impossible to completely eradicate the parasite. Only one human illness has been entirely eradicated: smallpox. 3c0d1c54d2b9.title: Two simple eating habits linked to lower weight, study finds
3c0d1c54d2b9.summary: A major study suggests that when you eat could play a key role in staying lean. People who fast longer overnight and start their day with an early breakfast were more likely to have a lower BMI years later. Scientists think this is because eating earlier aligns better with the body’s internal clock. But skipping breakfast as part of intermittent fasting didn’t offer the same advantage—and may even be tied to unhealthy habits.

2f186a8b202a.summary: Scientists have achieved the unthinkable by stabilizing a highly reactive molecule in water, confirming a decades-old theory about vitamin B1’s role in the body. The breakthrough not only solves a scientific mystery but could revolutionize greener chemical manufacturing.

e9382d42c2a8.title: Your nose could detect Alzheimer’s years before symptoms begin
e9382d42c2a8.summary: Losing your sense of smell might signal Alzheimer’s far earlier than expected. Scientists found that immune cells in the brain actively destroy smell-related nerve fibers after detecting abnormal signals on their surfaces. This damage begins in early stages of the disease, well before cognitive decline. The discovery could help identify at-risk patients sooner and improve treatment timing.

01badcf2a4ca.title: Early weight gain is linked to lifelong health consequences
01badcf2a4ca.summary: Putting on weight earlier in life may be more dangerous than previously thought. Researchers found that early adulthood obesity significantly raises the risk of premature death, especially from major diseases like heart disease and diabetes. The longer the body carries excess weight, the greater the damage appears to be. Interestingly, cancer risk in women didn’t follow this pattern, suggesting other biological factors are at play.

8143661509e9.title: A common nutrient could supercharge cancer treatment
8143661509e9.summary: A common eye-health nutrient, zeaxanthin, may also help the body fight cancer more effectively. Scientists discovered it strengthens T cells and enhances the impact of immunotherapy treatments. Found in everyday vegetables and supplements, it’s safe, accessible, and shows strong potential as a cancer therapy booster. Human trials are the next step.

1a497306483b.title: Goodbye colonoscopy? New stool test detects 90% of colorectal cancers
1a497306483b.summary: Новое исследование в области микробиома может привести к замене колоноскопии на анализ кала для выявления рака толстой кишки. Ученые с помощью искусственного интеллекта обнаружили связь между специфическими бактериальными паттернами в кале и развитием рака, достигнув точности в 90%.  Этот метод потенциально может стать более удобным и доступным способом скрининга этого распространенного заболевания.
1a497306483b.original_description: A breakthrough in microbiome research could change how colorectal cancer is detected—no colonoscopy required. Scientists used AI to map gut bacteria at an unprecedented level of detail, revealing subtle microbial patterns linked to cancer. By analyzing simple stool samples, their method identified 90% of cases, rivaling one of medicine’s most trusted diagnostic tools.

daebae21cad5.title: Scientists say we’ve been treating Alzheimer’s all wrong
daebae21cad5.summary: Alzheimer’s isn’t just one problem—it’s a tangled mix of biology, aging, and overall health. That’s why drugs targeting a single factor have fallen short, even as new treatments show modest benefits. Scientists are now pushing toward multi-pronged strategies, from gene editing to brain-cell rejuvenation and gut health interventions. The goal: stop treating Alzheimer’s as one disease and start tackling it as a complex system.

a3b0a7a5d1b1.title: Gravitational waves may be hidden in the light atoms emit
a3b0a7a5d1b1.summary: Scientists have proposed a surprising new way to detect gravitational waves—by observing how they change the light emitted by atoms. These waves can subtly shift photon frequencies in different directions, leaving behind a detectable signature. The effect doesn’t change how much light atoms emit, which is why it’s gone unnoticed until now. If confirmed, this approach could lead to ultra-compact detectors using cold-atom systems.

d6b575b88bd7.title: This superconductivity dies then comes back to life
d6b575b88bd7.summary: A strange new kind of superconductivity has been uncovered in uranium ditelluride (UTe2), where electricity flows with zero resistance—but only under extremely strong magnetic fields that should normally destroy it. Even more surprising, the superconductivity disappears at first and then dramatically reappears at even higher fields, earning it the nickname the “Lazarus phase.”

6c7e93a4e113.title: Scientists finally crack mystery of rare COVID vaccine blood clots
6c7e93a4e113.summary: Ученые выяснили механизм редкого случая образования тромбов после некоторых вакцин от COVID-19 и заражения аденовирусами: иммунная система ошибочно атакует нормальный белок крови.  Это открытие позволит разрабатывать новые, более безопасные вакцины, сохраняя их эффективность.
6c7e93a4e113.original_description: Researchers have uncovered why a rare blood clotting disorder can occur after certain COVID-19 vaccines or adenovirus infections. The immune system can mistakenly target a normal blood protein (PF4) after confusing it with a viral protein. This triggers clotting in extremely rare cases. The breakthrough means vaccines can now be redesigned to avoid this reaction while staying effective.

4e234ed4d203.title: These cheap solar cells work better because they’re flawed
4e234ed4d203.summary: Perovskite solar cells shouldn’t work as well as they do—but they do. Scientists have now discovered that defects inside the material actually help, creating networks that separate and guide electric charges efficiently. Using a novel imaging method, they revealed hidden structures acting like charge “highways.” This insight could unlock even more powerful, low-cost solar cells.

cebbb0341af3.title: This new chip could slash data center energy waste
cebbb0341af3.summary: A new chip design from UC San Diego could make data centers far more energy-efficient by rethinking how power is converted for GPUs. By combining vibrating piezoelectric components with a clever circuit layout, the system overcomes limitations of traditional designs. The prototype achieved impressive efficiency and delivered much more power than previous attempts. Though not ready for widespread use yet, it points to a promising future for high-performance computing.

64ed21f2b5ab.title: Scientists think dark matter might come in two forms
64ed21f2b5ab.summary: A mysterious glow of gamma rays at the center of the Milky Way has long hinted at dark matter, but the lack of similar signals in smaller dwarf galaxies has cast doubt on that idea. Now, researchers propose a bold twist: dark matter might not be a single particle at all, but a mix of two different types that must interact with each other to produce detectable signals.

5cb6f8215026.title: Dragonflies can see a color humans can’t and it could change medicine
5cb6f8215026.summary: Dragonflies may see the world in a way that pushes beyond human limits—and surprisingly, they do it using the same molecular trick we evolved ourselves. Scientists discovered that these insects can detect extremely deep red light, even edging into near-infrared, thanks to a specialized visual protein strikingly similar to the one in human eyes. This ability likely helps them spot mates mid-flight by picking up subtle differences in reflected light.

77ddbebd54ea.title: The world is getting brighter at night but some places are going dark
77ddbebd54ea.summary: В целом, ночи на Земле становятся ярче, но эта тенденция сильно различается в разных регионах. Быстрый рост городов, особенно в Китае и Индии, увеличивает освещенность, в то время как в Европе наблюдается уменьшение освещенности благодаря энергосберегающим мерам. Анализ спутниковых данных показывает, что эти изменения происходят быстрее и неравномернее, чем предполагалось, с локальными контрастами, обусловленными различными факторами.
77ddbebd54ea.original_description: Earth’s nights are steadily getting brighter overall, but the changes vary dramatically by region. Rapid urban growth is lighting up countries like China and India, while parts of Europe are dimming due to energy-saving efforts and new lighting technologies. The most detailed satellite analysis yet shows these shifts happening faster and more unevenly than expected. Even global trends can mask sharp local contrasts, from war-related blackouts to deliberate reductions in light pollution.

daf227d42b8b.title: Scientists finally uncover why promising cancer drugs keep failing
daf227d42b8b.summary: Новое исследование объясняет, почему перспективные противораковые препараты, ингибиторы BET, часто неэффективны в клинической практике. Оказалось, что два связанных белка, BRD2 и BRD4, выполняют разные функции: BRD2 подготавливает гены к активации, а BRD4 инициирует этот процесс. Блокирование обоих белков одновременно может приводить к непредсказуемым нарушениям в регуляции генов, объясняя неудачу препаратов.
daf227d42b8b.original_description: Cancer drugs known as BET inhibitors once looked like a breakthrough, but in real patients they’ve often fallen short. New research reveals a key reason why: two closely related proteins, BRD2 and BRD4, don’t actually do the same job. Instead, BRD2 acts like a “stage manager,” preparing genes for activation, while BRD4 triggers the final step that turns them on. By blocking both at once, current drugs may be disrupting the process in unpredictable ways.

7b1909fd0c5e.title: Breakthrough water filter removes 98% of toxic PFAS forever chemicals
7b1909fd0c5e.summary: Ученые разработали новый метод фильтрации воды, использующий нано-клетки для улавливания "вечных химикатов" PFAS.  Технология эффективно удаляет до 98% этих загрязнителей, включая трудноудаляемые короткоцепочечные PFAS, и сохраняет свою эффективность при многократном использовании. Это открытие может привести к созданию более эффективных систем очистки воды по всему миру.
7b1909fd0c5e.original_description: Scientists have developed a clever new way to trap “forever chemicals” in water using nano-sized cages that lock onto PFAS molecules. Unlike current methods, this approach can capture short-chain PFAS—the hardest type to remove. Tests show it can eliminate up to 98% of these pollutants and still work after multiple uses. The discovery could lead to more effective water filtration systems worldwide.

ce4416f0e7ab.title: Scientists discover spice synergy that boosts anti-inflammation 100x
ce4416f0e7ab.summary: Chronic inflammation often works quietly in the background but can fuel serious diseases like diabetes, heart disease, and cancer. New research reveals that everyday plant compounds—like menthol from mint, cineole from eucalyptus, and capsaicin from chili peppers—can team up inside immune cells to dramatically boost their anti-inflammatory power. While individual compounds showed modest effects, certain combinations amplified results hundreds of times over by activating different cellular pathwa

c176a440f423.title: The hidden tradeoff behind today’s most popular weight loss drugs
c176a440f423.summary: Новое исследование показывает, что популярные препараты для похудения и бариатрическая хирургия, несмотря на разный механизм действия, приводят к сходным физиологическим изменениям: снижению жировой массы и умеренной потере мышечной массы, что влияет на общую структуру тела.  Хотя эти методы улучшают здоровье, важно учитывать, что потеря мышечной массы может снизить защиту от преждевременной смерти, тем самым представляя собой компромисс.
c176a440f423.original_description: Weight loss drugs and bariatric surgery may work differently, but they lead to surprisingly similar results inside the body. Both significantly reduce fat while also causing a modest loss of muscle, reshaping overall body composition. Since muscle helps protect against early death, this balance matters more than the number on the scale. The study suggests these treatments improve health—but not without trade-offs.
